verdict: Retargeting spend is steady but we're flying blind without lead, conversion, or revenue data to judge results.
diagnosis: Vous Physique spent $1,062.69 on one Facebook retargeting campaign this period, with daily spend dropping from $22.61 average to $12.28 latest.

signals.headline: Total spend hit $1,062.69 this period
signals.detail: All ad budget ran through one Facebook retargeting campaign. No diversification across prospecting or other channels.

signals.headline: Daily spend fell 46% to $12.28
signals.detail: Latest daily spend is well below the $22.61 period average. Budget pacing is slowing, not scaling.

signals.headline: Zero lead or revenue data tracked
signals.detail: No conversions, calls, or pipeline tied to spend. Impossible to judge if $1,062.69 produced any members.

signals.headline: Only 1 campaign running across 28 days
signals.detail: Retargeting alone cannot fill the funnel. Without cold prospecting, the retargeting pool dries up.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.body: Boutique studio cost per lead typically lands between $25 and $50 on Meta. Fitness conversion rates on lead forms reach 14%, the highest of any vertical. Vous Physique has no lead data to compare.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.subheader: May–June is a cheap window for fitness ads
seasonal.body: April and May are the cheapest months of the year for fitness lead costs, running up to 64% below the global average. July spikes sharply. The window to acquire cheaply is closing fast.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.body: Boutique operators are pulling spend from acquisition toward member retention, community events, and Pilates or hybrid strength formats. Studios with strong community retain members 30–50% longer.

recommendations.title: Install lead and revenue tracking immediately
recommendations.detail: Connect Facebook lead forms or website conversions back to spend. Without this, every other decision is a guess. Worth doing before adding any new budget.

recommendations.title: Test a cold prospecting campaign alongside retargeting
recommendations.detail: Retargeting alone shrinks over time. Consider a small prospecting test with a low-friction offer like a free week or intro class while June costs stay low.

recommendations.title: Lock in spend before July cost spike
recommendations.detail: Fitness ad costs jump sharply in July. Consider front-loading June budget to capture cheaper leads before the seasonal increase hits.
